title: Reverse Linked List
prompt:
Given the head of a singly linked list represented as an array, reverse the list and return the reversed list as an array.

For example, the array `[1, 2, 3, 4, 5]` represents the linked list `1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5`. Reversing it yields `5 -> 4 -> 3 -> 2 -> 1`, returned as `[5, 4, 3, 2, 1]`.

An empty array represents an empty list.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Iterate through the array and build a new reversed array, or use two-pointer swap from both ends. For a true linked list approach, maintain prev/curr pointers and reverse links one by one.
commonBugs: Not handling the empty list case
Off-by-one when swapping with two pointers
Forgetting to return the result instead of modifying in place
